perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, EnCana agrees that the Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and related actions, and that such information will be kept confidential by EnCana and its Representatives; provided, however, that (w) such information may be disclosed by EnCana to its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction or their participation therein (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information), (x) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement if any disclosure of such information is made with TBI's prior written consent, (y) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that EnCana believes, based on the advice of counsel, that it is legally required to disclose such information in order to avoid committing a violation of any law, rule or regulation, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system, provided that EnCana Ex. (e)(3)-1 provides prompt notice to TBI of the proposed disclosure and takes the other actions required in connection with a required disclosure pursuant to Section 3 below and (z) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that such disclosure is permitted pursuant to Section 3 below.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, EnCana agrees that the Evaluation Material may be used for any purpose, and that such information may or may not be kept confidential by EnCana and its Representatives; provided, however, that (w) such information may be disclosed by EnCana to its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction or their participation therein (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information), (x) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement if any disclosure of such information is made with TBI's prior written consent, (y) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that EnCana believes, based on the advice of counsel, that it is legally required to disclose such information in order to avoid committing a violation of any law, rule or regulation, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system, provided that EnCana Ex. (e)(3)-1 provides prompt notice to TBI of the proposed disclosure and takes the other actions required in connection with a required disclosure pursuant to Section 3 below and (z) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that such disclosure is permitted pursuant to Section 3 below.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction" to "may be used for any purpose" and "will be kept confidential" to "may or may not be kept confidential", this contradicts the entire purpose of a confidentiality agreement. This change introduces a significant level of uncertainty as the original purpose of the Evaluation material was to be kept confidential. The change to "may or may not be kept confidential" allows for Encana to disclose this information as they please, which contradicts the nature of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representatives " shall mean such party's affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, lenders or advisers and representatives of the foregoing. EnCana recognizes and acknowledges the potential competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result from the disclosure thereof to third parties. Accordingly, EnCana agrees that the Evaluation Material may be used for any purpose, and that such information may or may not be kept confidential by EnCana and its Representatives; provided, however, that (w) such information may be disclosed by EnCana to its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction or their participation therein (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information), (x) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement if any disclosure of such information is made with TBI's prior written consent, (y) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that EnCana believes, based on the advice of counsel, that it is legally required to disclose such information in order to avoid committing a violation of any law, rule or regulation, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system, provided that EnCana Ex. (e)(3)-1 provides prompt notice to TBI of the proposed disclosure and takes the other actions required in connection with a required disclosure pursuant to Section 3 below and (z) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that such disclosure is permitted pursuant to Section 3 below. EnCana agrees to be responsible for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction" to "may be used for any purpose" and "will be kept confidential" to "may or may not be kept confidential." This directly contradicts the first sentence in the Contradicted Text: 'EnCana recognizes and acknowledges the potential competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result from the disclosure thereof to third parties.' By making the Evaluation Material able to be used for any purpose and potentially not kept confidential, this goes against the acknowledgement of competitive value and damage from disclosure. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, such party and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person (other than such party's Representative's on a need to know basis) the fact that any Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the possible Transaction (including the status thereof); provided, that a party may make such disclosure if (i) based on the advice of such party's counsel, such disclosure is necessary to avoid committing a violation of, or to insure compliance with, any laws, rules or regulations, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system and (ii) the disclosing party provides advance notice to the other party of the proposed disclosure and cooperates in good faith with respect to the timing, manner and content of such disclosure (to the extent consistent with its obligation to make disclosure).
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, with or without the prior written consent of the other party, such party and its Representatives may disclose to any other person (other than such party's Representative's on a need to know basis) the fact that any Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the possible Transaction (including the status thereof); provided, that a party may make such disclosure if (i) based on the advice of such party's counsel, such disclosure is necessary to avoid committing a violation of, or to insure compliance with, any laws, rules or regulations, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system and (ii) the disclosing party provides advance notice to the other party of the proposed disclosure and cooperates in good faith with respect to the timing, manner and content of such disclosure (to the extent consistent with its obligation to make disclosure).
perturbation.explanation: By changing "without the prior written consent" to "with or without the prior written consent" the confidentiality of discussions is undermined, creating a direct contradiction to the purpose of the section. This change completely voids the requirement for consent, rendering the rest of the clause's stipulations less meaningful and contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that, with or without the prior written consent of the other party, such party and its Representatives may disclose to any other person (other than such party's Representative's on a need to know basis) the fact that any Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the possible Transaction (including the status thereof); provided, that a party may make such disclosure if (i) based on the advice of such party's counsel, such disclosure is necessary to avoid committing a violation of, or to insure compliance with, any laws, rules or regulations, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system and (ii) the disclosing party provides advance notice to the other party of the proposed disclosure and cooperates in good faith with respect to the timing, manner and content of such disclosure (to the extent consistent with its obligation to make disclosure). 3. Required Disclosure. In the event that EnCana or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any other facts or information, the disclosure of which is prohibited by this letter agreement, EnCana shall (i) provide TBI with prompt notice of any such request or requirement so that TBI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement and (ii) consult with TBI as to the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality agreement by stating that a party 'may disclose' information 'with or without prior written consent,' which directly contradicts the original clause that requires 'prior written consent' for disclosure. This undermines the purpose of the confidentiality agreement stated in the explanation and conflicts with the contradicted text, which outlines a process for EnCana to notify TBI if disclosure is required by legal proceedings. By removing the requirement for prior consent, the change creates a significant inconsistency in the document's confidentiality provisions, as it allows for unilateral disclosure without the other party's agreement. The change makes it impossible to reconcile both sections since one permits free disclosure while the other expects some kind of prevention.

perturbation.original_text: EnCana shall (i) provide TBI with prompt notice of any such request or requirement so that TBI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement and (ii) consult with TBI as to the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request.
perturbation.changed_text: EnCana shall (i) not provide TBI with prompt notice of any such request or requirement so that TBI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement and (ii) not consult with TBI as to the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'provide' to 'not provide' fundamentally undermines the notice requirement in case of required disclosures. Section 3 requires Encana to notify TBI if it is legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material. Changing this to 'not provide' directly contradicts the intention of the section, which is to allow TBI to protect its confidential information. With this changes creates an in-text contradiction between the obligations outlined earlier in the agreement regarding confidentiality and the actions to be taken when disclosure is legally mandated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Required Disclosure. In the event that EnCana or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any other facts or information, the disclosure of which is prohibited by this letter agreement, EnCana shall (i) not provide TBI with prompt notice of any such request or requirement so that TBI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement and (ii) not consult with TBI as to the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy, or the receipt of a waiver by TBI, EnCana or any of its Representatives should nonetheless, based on the advice of such party's counsel, disclose the Evaluation Material and/or the facts or information covered by Section 2, EnCana or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information that such counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed; provided that EnCana gives TBI written notice of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as is reasonably practicable and exercises its reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or other information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with TBI to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Evaluation Material and/or such facts or information.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires EnCana to provide TBI with notice of any request to disclose Evaluation Material, allowing TBI to seek a protective order. The changed text explicitly states that EnCana shall NOT provide TBI with such notice. The contradicted text, 'Required Disclosure', reiterates this lack of notice, which directly contradicts the purpose of protecting confidential information as outlined earlier in the agreement. By negating the notice requirement, the change introduces a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will not continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the obligation to maintain confidentiality after termination of discussions, this amendment directly contradicts the fundamental intent of the confidentiality agreement. This alters the temporal scope of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction between the initial aim to protect information for 18 months post-termination and this new statement indicating no such obligation exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If requested, the destruction of Evaluation Material shall be certified in writing by an authorized officer of EnCana. Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will not continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof. Ex. (e)(3)-2 5. Standstill Agreement. For a period of 18 months from the date hereof, EnCana agrees that it and its affiliates shall not, without the prior written request of TBI, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, or otherwise, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of more than 1% of any class of voting securities issued by TBI or any material assets of TBI or any securities or material assets of any subsidiary of TBI, (b) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (c) make, or in any way participate in, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission) with respect to the securities of TBI, or advise or seek to influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, or giving of consents with respect to, any securities of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (d) seek or propose to influence or control (whether though a "group," as such term is used in Rule 13d-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or otherwise) the management, board of directors, policies or affairs of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (e) to the extent doing so would require the public disclosure of such action by TBI, make any request to waive or amend any provision of this Section 5, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing or (g) encourage any third party to do any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this Section 5 shall terminate with respect to EnCana if a person or entity (other than EnCana or an affiliate of EnCana) acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, directly or indirectly, by merger or otherwise, more than 50% of the voting securities of TBI (i.e., those securities of TBI that are entitled to participate in the annual election of directors), or otherwise acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, the ability to control the management or policies of TBI.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations continue for 18 months after termination of discussions. The changed text explicitly states that confidentiality obligations do NOT continue after termination of discussions. This directly contradicts the purpose of the original agreement to protect information during that 18 month period, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted section which mentions 'Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will not continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof' because in the Change Text, it is stating that the parties are not bound by this confidentiality agreement, which contradicts the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Until the first anniversary of the date hereof, EnCana will not, nor will it permit any of its subsidiaries, directors, officers or employees to, directly or indirectly, solicit or hire the services, as employee, consultant or otherwise, of any employee of TBI or its subsidiaries with whom EnCana had contact in connection with its consideration of the Transaction or who became known to EnCana specifically in connection with its consideration of the Transaction, except that this provision shall not prohibit the hiring of any such employee (i) who responds to any public advertising for employment without any other direct or indirect solicitation or (ii) whose employment by TBI or its subsidiary has been terminated prior to the commencement of discussions with such employee.
perturbation.changed_text: Until the first anniversary of the date hereof, EnCana will, nor will it permit any of its subsidiaries, directors, officers or employees to, directly or indirectly, solicit or hire the services, as employee, consultant or otherwise, of any employee of TBI or its subsidiaries with whom EnCana had contact in connection with its consideration of the Transaction or who became known to EnCana specifically in connection with its consideration of the Transaction, except that this provision shall not prohibit the hiring of any such employee (i) who responds to any public advertising for employment without any other direct or indirect solicitation or (ii) whose employment by TBI or its subsidiary has been terminated prior to the commencement of discussions with such employee.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "will not" to "will" this perturbation directly contradicts the original intent of this section of the document. This alteration creates a significant in-text contradiction, particularly in light of the agreement's objective to safeguard proprietary information and business interests during potential transactions. If Encana can, directly or indirectly, solicit or hire employees this goes against the objective of the confidentiality agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prohibition on Employee Hiring. Until the first anniversary of the date hereof, EnCana will, nor will it permit any of its subsidiaries, directors, officers or employees to, directly or indirectly, solicit or hire the services, as employee, consultant or otherwise, of any employee of TBI or its subsidiaries with whom EnCana had contact in connection with its consideration of the Transaction or who became known to EnCana specifically in connection with its consideration of the Transaction, except that this provision shall not prohibit the hiring of any such employee (i) who responds to any public advertising for employment without any other direct or indirect solicitation or (ii) whose employment by TBI or its subsidiary has been terminated prior to the commencement of discussions with such employee. 8. Securities Law. EnCana hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and that it has advised or will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters that arc the subject of this agreement, that the United States securities laws may prohibit any person who has material, nonpublic information concerning the matters that are the subject of this agreement from purchasing or selling securities of a company that may be a party to a transaction of the type contemplated by this agreement or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.justification: The change from "will not" to "will" directly contradicts the original intent of the section, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text is the same section as the Changed Text which makes the contradiction readily and easily visible. Originally, EnCana was prohibited from soliciting or hiring employees of TBI. The change now allows them to do so, creating a direct conflict. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition because the clause is essentially placed in the same location with conflicting implications.
